# 98.css (retro-98-css)

**Upstream:** https://github.com/jdan/98.css
**Demo:** https://jdan.github.io/98.css/ (also ./demo.html)
**License:** MIT (c) 2020 Jordan Scales — see LICENSE

## What it looks like
Windows 98 / NT4 aesthetic. 3D beveled buttons (raised/pressed), battleship-grey panels,
2px outset/inset borders, MS Sans Serif fonts (bundled as Pixelated MS Sans Serif), blue
title-bar gradient (#000080 -> #1084d0), pixel-perfect focus rectangles.

## What it offers
- Window chrome: `.window`, `.title-bar`, `.title-bar-text`, `.title-bar-controls`
- Buttons with proper 3D states (active, focus, disabled)
- Form controls: radios, checkboxes, text inputs, select, combobox
- Group-boxes (fieldset), tree-view, tabs, progress bars (segmented + solid)
- Menu-bar + dropdown, scrollbars (requires webkit)
- Single style.css import. Uses CSS custom properties for theming.

## Clawmachine fit
Strong candidate for "operator inspector / debugging view" chrome.
3D bevel language is the anti-thesis to Aero glass - aligns with no-frosted-glass doctrine.
